data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260509T225401-286e8c4e/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260509T225401-286e8c4e/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260509T225401-286e8c4e/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260509T225401-286e8c4e/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260509T225401-286e8c4e/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-f93b0762">
    <title>agent/store: extract Store.ParkToProposed helper for the 6 duplicate sites</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  Six call sites in cli/internal/agent/ each implement the same
  "park bead to proposed for operator attention" pattern: status transition
  open|in_progress -> proposed via UpdateWithLifecycleStatus with
  OperatorRequired=true plus a Reason and Source string, then a mutate
  closure to set Extra fields. The boilerplate duplication makes it easy
  to forget OperatorRequired=true (the validator would reject the call,
  but the failure is far from the call site) or to set inconsistent
  Source strings.

ROOT CAUSE
  Six near-identical call sites:
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2408 parkBeadPostIntakeRejection
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2475 applyNoChangesSmartRetry
    (open path, comparison reference)
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2501 applyNoChangesOperatorRequired
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2532 applyNoChangesBlockedExternal
    (blocked path, comparison reference; not a "park" but uses the same
    UpdateWithLifecycleStatus shape)
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:568 review malfunction
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_terminal.go:78 review terminal error
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:229 git merge conflict

  Each repeats the LifecycleTransitionOptions{OperatorRequired: true,
  Reason: ..., Source: ...} initializer plus a mutate function that sets
  one or more Extra fields.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add Store.ParkToProposed in cli/internal/bead/store.go (or a new file
    under cli/internal/bead/) with signature:
        func (s *Store) ParkToProposed(id string, reason ParkReason,
            mutate func(*Bead)) error
    where ParkReason is a string-typed enum (or struct) with constants:
        ParkIntakeRejection
        ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired
        ParkPostReviewMalfunction
        ParkReviewTerminal
        ParkConflictRecovery
    Each constant maps internally to a (Reason, Source) pair so call sites
    only pass the enum.
  - Convert the 5 actual park call sites (excluding the comparison-only
    sites at 2475 and 2532) to call ParkToProposed.
  - Add a unit test TestParkToProposed_TransitionAndMetadata in
    cli/internal/bead/store_test.go asserting:
      a. Park transitions open|in_progress -> proposed.
      b. The mutate callback runs after the transition.
      c. The Reason/Source pair matches the ParkReason mapping.
      d. Park does NOT change status when called on a terminal-status bead
         (relies on existing ValidateLifecycleTransition).

NON-SCOPE
  - Changing the underlying lifecycle gate or validator.
  - Touching applyNoChangesSmartRetry (-> open) or applyNoChangesBlockedExternal
    (-> blocked); they are not park-to-proposed and are listed only as
    comparison reference.
  - Removing or renaming the existing helper functions that wrap each
    call site; they may keep their public signatures and just delegate.
  - Adding ParkToProposed to the agent package; the helper belongs in
    cli/internal/bead/ alongside the rest of the lifecycle API.

INTERSECTIONS
  - ddx-ca1c66ee (epic parent).
  - No deps on B1/B2/B3/B4; can run in parallel.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Store.ParkToProposed exists in cli/internal/bead/store.go (or a sibling file under cli/internal/bead/) with the signature documented in PROPOSED FIX, taking (id string, reason ParkReason, mutate func(*Bead)) error.
2. ParkReason enum constants exist for ParkIntakeRejection, ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired, ParkPostReviewMalfunction, ParkReviewTerminal, ParkConflictRecovery.
3. The 5 park call sites (execute_bead_loop.go:2408, execute_bead_loop.go:2501, execute_bead_post_review.go:568, execute_bead_review_terminal.go:78, execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go:229) call Store.ParkToProposed instead of inlining UpdateWithLifecycleStatus + LifecycleTransitionOptions.
4. TestParkToProposed_TransitionAndMetadata in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go asserts the four behavioral properties listed in PROPOSED FIX (transition, mutate, reason/source mapping, terminal-status rejection).
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, area:bead, kind:refactor, spec:TD-031</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-ca1c66ee" spec-id="TD-031" base-rev="e64f875b397e79b9e95e22c8373bc37619d67004" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260509T225401-286e8c4e"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="TD-031" path="docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md">Technical Design: Bead State Machine and Naming-Role Decisions</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Commit succeeded. All ACs are satisfied:

1. **`Store.ParkToProposed`** exists in `cli/internal/bead/park.go` with signature `(id string, reason ParkReason, mutate func(*Bead)) error`.
2. **`ParkReason` constants** — `ParkIntakeRejection`, `ParkNoChangesOperatorRequired`, `ParkPostReviewMalfunction`, `ParkReviewTerminal`, `ParkConflictRecovery` — all defined.
3. **5 call sites converted** — `execute_bead_loop.go` (parkBeadPostIntakeRejection + applyNoChangesOperatorRequired), `execute_bead_post_review.go` (applyTriageAction), `execute_bead_review_terminal.go` (applyReviewOperatorRequiredParking), `execute_bead_conflict_recovery.go` (land conflict).
4. **`TestParkToProposed_TransitionAndMetadata`** in `store_test.go` with all 4 behavioral properties (transition, mutate-after, reason/source map, terminal rejection).
5. All tests green: `./internal/bead/...`, `./internal/agent/...`, `./cmd/...`.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
